## Student Care Services

### ER Diagram

```mermaid
erDiagram
  COMPLAINT_CATEGORY {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string description
    integer escalation_days
  }
  COMPLAINT {
    uuid id PK
    uuid category_id FK
    string student_id
    string description
    string status
    timestamp created_at
    timestamp updated_at
  }
  REPLY_TEMPLATE {
    uuid id PK
    uuid category_id FK
    string template
    boolean is_standard
  }
  TELEPHONIC_QUERY {
    uuid id PK
    string student_id
    string query
    timestamp logged_at
  }
  ALERT {
    uuid id PK
    string message
    string recipient
    string medium
    timestamp sent_at
  }
  COMPLAINT_CATEGORY ||--o{ COMPLAINT : contains
  COMPLAINT_CATEGORY ||--o{ REPLY_TEMPLATE : has
  COMPLAINT ||--o{ TELEPHONIC_QUERY : logs
  COMPLAINT ||--o{ ALERT : triggers
```

### Tables

#### `complaint_category`

Stores categories for complaints.

| Column | Type | Constraints | Justification |
|--------|------|-------------|---------------|
| `id` | `uuid` | PRIMARY KEY | Unique identifier for each complaint category. |
| `name` | `string` | NOT NULL | Name of the complaint category. |
| `description` | `string` | NULL | Description of the complaint category. |
| `escalation_days` | `integer` | NOT NULL CHECK (escalation_days >= 0) | Number of days for escalation as per SRS. |
| `escalation_level_1` | `string` | NOT NULL | Details for escalation level 1. |
| `escalation_level_2` | `string` | NULL | Details for escalation level 2. |

**Indexes:** `idx_complaint_category_name`

#### `complaint`

Stores individual complaints submitted by students.

| Column | Type | Constraints | Justification |
|--------|------|-------------|---------------|
| `id` | `uuid` | PRIMARY KEY | Unique identifier for each complaint. |
| `category_id` | `uuid` | NOT NULL REFERENCES complaint_category(id) | Foreign key linking to the complaint category. |
| `student_id` | `string` | NOT NULL | Identifier for the student submitting the complaint. |
| `description` | `string` | NOT NULL | Detailed description of the complaint. |
| `status` | `string` | NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('Pending', 'Resolved', 'Escalated')) | Current status of the complaint. |
| `created_at` | `timestamp` | DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP | Timestamp when the complaint was created. |
| `updated_at` | `timestamp` | DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP | Timestamp when the complaint was last updated. |

**Indexes:** `idx_complaint_student_id`

#### `reply_template`

Stores standard reply templates for complaints.

| Column | Type | Constraints | Justification |
|--------|------|-------------|---------------|
| `id` | `uuid` | PRIMARY KEY | Unique identifier for each reply template. |
| `category_id` | `uuid` | NOT NULL REFERENCES complaint_category(id) | Foreign key linking to the complaint category. |
| `template` | `string` | NOT NULL | The content of the reply template. |
| `is_standard` | `boolean` | DEFAULT TRUE | Indicates if the template is a standard reply. |

**Indexes:** `idx_reply_template_category_id`

#### `telephonic_query`

Logs telephonic queries made by students.

| Column | Type | Constraints | Justification |
|--------|------|-------------|---------------|
| `id` | `uuid` | PRIMARY KEY | Unique identifier for each telephonic query. |
| `student_id` | `string` | NOT NULL | Identifier for the student making the query. |
| `query` | `string` | NOT NULL | Content of the telephonic query. |
| `logged_at` | `timestamp` | DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP | Timestamp when the query was logged. |

**Indexes:** `idx_telephonic_query_student_id`

#### `alert`

Stores alerts sent to students.

| Column | Type | Constraints | Justification |
|--------|------|-------------|---------------|
| `id` | `uuid` | PRIMARY KEY | Unique identifier for each alert. |
| `message` | `string` | NOT NULL | Content of the alert message. |
| `recipient` | `string` | NOT NULL | Recipient of the alert. |
| `medium` | `string` | NOT NULL CHECK (medium IN ('Email', 'SMS')) | Medium through which the alert is sent. |
| `sent_at` | `timestamp` | DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP | Timestamp when the alert was sent. |

**Indexes:** `idx_alert_recipient`

### Business Rules

| Rule ID | Description | SRS Reference |
|---------|-------------|---------------|
| BR_001 | Each complaint category must have a defined escalation process with specific timelines. | 12.1.2.1 |
| BR_002 | Standard reply templates must be associated with specific complaint types. | 12.2.1 |
| BR_003 | Telephonic queries must be logged by designated personnel. | 12.3.1 |
| BR_004 | Alerts must be sent via email or SMS for specific events. | 12.4.2 |

### API Endpoints

| Method | Path | Description | SRS Reference |
|--------|------|-------------|---------------|
| `GET` | `/api/v1/complaint_categories` | Retrieve a list of all complaint categories. | 12.1.1 |
| `POST` | `/api/v1/complaint_categories` | Create a new complaint category. | 12.1.1 |
| `GET` | `/api/v1/complaints` | Retrieve a list of all complaints. | 12.1.1 |
| `POST` | `/api/v1/complaints` | Create a new complaint. | 12.1.1 |
| `PATCH` | `/api/v1/complaints/{id}/status` | Update the status of a complaint. | 12.1.1 |
| `GET` | `/api/v1/reply_templates` | Retrieve a list of all reply templates. | 12.2.1 |
| `POST` | `/api/v1/reply_templates` | Create a new reply template. | 12.2.1 |
| `GET` | `/api/v1/telephonic_queries` | Retrieve a list of all telephonic queries. | 12.3.1 |
| `POST` | `/api/v1/telephonic_queries` | Log a new telephonic query. | 12.3.1 |
| `GET` | `/api/v1/alerts` | Retrieve a list of all alerts. | 12.4.1 |
| `POST` | `/api/v1/alerts` | Create a new alert. | 12.4.1 |

### Workflows

#### Complaint Status Management *(SRS: 12.1.1)*

**States:** `Pending` → `Resolved` → `Escalated`

| From | To | Trigger | API Endpoint |
|------|----|---------|--------------|
| `Pending` | `Resolved` | resolve_complaint | `PATCH /api/v1/complaints/{id}/status` |
| `Pending` | `Escalated` | escalate_complaint | `PATCH /api/v1/complaints/{id}/status` |
| `Escalated` | `Resolved` | resolve_escalated_complaint | `PATCH /api/v1/complaints/{id}/status` |

#### Employee Status Update *(SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.7)*

**States:** `probation` → `confirmed` → `resigned` → `terminated` → `suspended` → `deputation` → `retired` → `reemployed`

| From | To | Trigger | API Endpoint |
|------|----|---------|--------------|

| `confirmed` | `resigned` | resign employee | `PATCH /api/v1/employees/{id}/status` |
| `confirmed` | `terminated` | terminate employee | `PATCH /api/v1/employees/{id}/status` |

systemDesignMarkdown:
To design a high-level system architecture based on the provided module summaries, we will create a microservices architecture that allows for scalability, maintainability, and separation of concerns. Each module will be represented as a microservice with its own API endpoints, business rules, and workflows. The architecture will include an API Gateway for routing requests, a Message Queue for asynchronous communication between services, and a database for each module.

### High-Level System Architecture

1. **API Gateway**: The API Gateway will serve as the entry point for all client requests. It will route requests to the appropriate microservices based on the endpoint being accessed.

2. **Microservices**: Each module will be implemented as a separate microservice:
   - Admission Service
   - Fee Management Service
   - Calendar Management Service
   - Academics Service
   - Time Table Management Service
   - Attendance Management Service
   - Examination Service
   - Placement Portal Service
   - Alumni Portal Service
   - Research Service
   - Student Care Services Service
   - Human Resource Service
   - Finance Service
   - Accounts Service

3. **Message Queue**: A message queue (e.g., RabbitMQ, Kafka) will be used for asynchronous communication between services. This will help in decoupling services and handling events like admissions, fee payments, attendance updates, etc.

4. **Databases**: Each microservice will have its own database schema to manage its data independently. This allows for better data management and reduces the risk of data conflicts.

5. **Authentication and Authorization**: Implement a centralized authentication service (e.g., OAuth2, JWT) to manage user access across all services.

### API Gateway Routing

The API Gateway will route requests based on the following patterns:

- `/admission/*` -> Admission Service
- `/fee-management/*` -> Fee Management Service
- `/calendar/*` -> Calendar Management Service
- `/academics/*` -> Academics Service
- `/timetable/*` -> Time Table Management Service
- `/attendance/*` -> Attendance Management Service
- `/examination/*` -> Examination Service
- `/placement/*` -> Placement Portal Service
- `/alumni/*` -> Alumni Portal Service
- `/research/*` -> Research Service
- `/student-care/*` -> Student Care Services Service
- `/hr/*` -> Human Resource Service
- `/finance/*` -> Finance Service
- `/accounts/*` -> Accounts Service

### Message Queue Event Flows

1. **Admission Process**:
   - Admission Service processes a new admission application.
   - It sends an event to the Message Queue (e.g., `admission.created`).
   - Other services (e.g., Fee Management, Academics) listen for this event to perform necessary actions (e.g., create a fee plan, register the student).

2. **Fee Payment**:
   - Fee Management Service processes a fee payment.
   - It sends an event to the Message Queue (e.g., `fee.payment.completed`).
   - Other services (e.g., Accounts, Finance) listen for this event to update their records.

3. **Attendance Update**:
   - Attendance Management Service updates attendance records.
   - It sends an event to the Message Queue (e.g., `attendance.updated`).
   - Academics Service listens for this event to update student performance metrics.

### Mermaid Architecture Diagram

```mermaid
graph TD;
    A[API Gateway] -->|Routes| B[Admission Service]
    A -->|Routes| C[Fee Management Service]
    A -->|Routes| D[Calendar Management Service]
    A -->|Routes| E[Academics Service]
    A -->|Routes| F[Time Table Management Service]
    A -->|Routes| G[Attendance Management Service]
    A -->|Routes| H[Examination Service]
    A -->|Routes| I[Placement Portal Service]
    A -->|Routes| J[Alumni Portal Service]
    A -->|Routes| K[Research Service]
    A -->|Routes| L[Student Care Services Service]
    A -->|Routes| M[Human Resource Service]
    A -->|Routes| N[Finance Service]
    A -->|Routes| O[Accounts Service]

    B -->|Event| P[Message Queue]
    C -->|Event| P
    D -->|Event| P
    E -->|Event| P
    F -->|Event| P
    G -->|Event| P
    H -->|Event| P
    I -->|Event| P
    J -->|Event| P
    K -->|Event| P
    L -->|Event| P
    M -->|Event| P
    N -->|Event| P
    O -->|Event| P
```

### Conclusion

This architecture provides a clear separation of concerns, allowing each module to operate independently while still being able to communicate through the API Gateway and Message Queue. This design will facilitate scalability and maintainability as the system grows and evolves.

Flow of ERP Solution(Version 3.0) 
1. Module Name-Admission Module 
1.1. Campaign Management 
1.1.1. Track the various campaigns run by the PR department. These could include 
purely internal campaigns run for initiatives among the students of the 
organization as well as marketing campaigns meant for interesting prospective 
students 
1.1.2. Permits campaign to run using one or more media channels like TV, radio, SMS 
,newspaper, bulletin boards etc. 
1.1.3. Enter the academy location programs covered 
1.2. Pre Admission Management 
1.2.1. Admission Notification for Online Application 
1.2.2. OTP based verification of mobile and email ID. 
1.3. Post Admission Management 
1.3.1. Option 1:Direct entry on restricted user privileges' and on
 restricted IP's. 
1.3.2. Option 2: student will fill the details and admission in-charge will approve
 the details 
1.3.3. Transformation of preadmission records to post-admission. 
1.4. Admission Enquiry Management 
1.4.1. Handle multiple prospective students in a single enquiry form but each 
prospective student will be assigned a separate enquiry id 
1.4.2. Record more than one program and batch of interest to a prospective student 
enquiring 
1.4.3. Record the prospective student details, exams passed or program studying in, 
details of siblings studying in the organization if any, details of parents, address for 
communication, whether the parent is an employee etc 
1.4.4. Follow up with enquiries. 
1.4.5. Direct Conversion of Enquiry to application and Application to Admission 
1.4.6. Application Registration by Prospect Student through online portal 
1.4.7. Conducting online entrance examination 
1.4.8. Allocation of students to various institutes. 
1.4.9. Registration Status Marking (Accept / Reject / Cancelled) 
1.4.10. Generate Merit list 
1.4.11. View / Edit Merit list

(Software Requirement Specification) 
GU-SRS Page 2 
1.4.12. Admission Process Management  Admission process management (round creation, 
reporting, document verification, fees submission, branch allotment, final confirmation) 
1.4.13. Admission Transaction Student wise Status 
1.4.14. Student Admission Page ( with Min Information/requirement ) 
1.4.15. Admission Confirmation Student wise / Bulk 
1.4.16. Offer Letter - Student Wise / Bulk  
1.4.17. Admission cancellation 
1.4.18. Generate Registration / Enrollment Number 
1.4.19. Student Information Entry Page ( Detailed) 
1.4.20. Mobile number linked with Aadhar card (student and parents) 
1.4.21. Progress bar in student login which shows status of on -going admission (in which 
phase student is currently in). 
1.4.22. Bulk Student Photo Upload Utility 
1.4.23. Provision for provisional admission and then confirmation to final admission 
1.4.24. Transformation of preadmission records to post-admission. 
1.4.25. Student Id card Generation 
1.4.26. Branch Change 
1.4.27. Admission Register Report and Admission Analytics 
1.4.28. Application Data Import Utility

1.4.31. Reports….

1.5.1. Student /Faculty/Employee can request  and upload  certificates that he/she 
wants to get issued. Auto approval, manual approval; rejection and complete 
certificate/document issue management can be done. 
1.5.2. Generate document using document generator and Print the 
certificates/documents directly on Institute stationary or pre -defined format is also 
present. 
1.5.3. Documents handed and upload over at time of admission. 
1.5.4. These could include: Domicile Certificate, Birth Certificate, Mark Sheets, Entrance 
Exam Certificate

12. Module Name-Student Care Services 
Student Grievances 
12.1. Admin Login 
12.1.1. System has option to create categories and sub categories under which 
complaints have to be accepted 
12.1.2. For each category and sub category system should provide configuration option 
for the to be accepted. following items 
12.1.2.1. Answered by 
12.1.2.2. Escalation Level 1 
12.1.2.3. Escalation Level 2 (optional) 
12.1.2.4. Reviewer (optional) 
12.1.2.5. Response from student after resolution (optional) 
For each of these, number of working days in which escalation will be done has to 
be captured. 
12.2. Standard reply templates 
12.2.1. System allows storing of standard reply templates with respect to each complaint

api_endpoints: GET /api/v1/campaigns - Retrieve a list of all campaigns.
POST /api/v1/campaigns - Create a new campaign.
GET /api/v1/prospective_students - Retrieve a list of all prospective students.
POST /api/v1/prospective_students - Create a new prospective student.
GET /api/v1/admission_applications - Retrieve a list of all admission applications.
POST /api/v1/admission_applications - Create a new admission application.
PATCH /api/v1/admission_applications/{id}/status - Update the status of an admission application.
GET /api/v1/admission_enquiries - Retrieve a list of all admission enquiries.
POST /api/v1/admission_enquiries - Create a new admission enquiry.
GET /api/v1/documents - Retrieve a list of all documents.
POST /api/v1/documents - Create a new document.
PATCH /api/v1/admission_applications/{id}/confirm - Confirm an admission application.
PATCH /api/v1/admission_applications/{id}/cancel - Cancel an admission application.

business_rules.description: Each prospective student must have a unique enquiry ID.
Each admission application must be linked to a specific campaign.
Admission status can only be Accept, Reject, or Cancelled.
Each student must have a unique registration number.
OTP verification is mandatory for mobile and email during application.

tables.description: Tracks various campaigns run by the PR department.
Stores details of prospective students.
Records admission applications from prospective students.
Records details of enquiries made by prospective students.
Stores merit list details for admission applications.
Stores documents submitted during the admission process.
Records confirmation of admission for students.
Records cancellation of admission applications.
Records follow-up actions for admission applications.
Junction table to handle many-to-many relationship between prospective students and their programs and batches of interest.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: New junction table for many-to-many relationship

tables.description: Handles the transformation of pre-admission records to post-admission.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: New table for managing record transformation

columns.name: admission_application_id
columns.justification: Links to the admission application being transformed

columns.name: status
columns.justification: Indicates the current status of the record

tables.description: Manages the admission process including round creation, reporting, document verification, fees submission, branch allotment, and final confirmation.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: New table for managing the admission process

columns.name: document_verified
columns.justification: Indicates if documents have been verified

columns.name: fees_submitted
columns.justification: Indicates if fees have been submitted

columns.name: branch_allotted
columns.justification: Records the branch allotted to the student

columns.name: final_confirmation
columns.justification: Indicates if the admission is confirmed

columns.name: created_at
columns.justification: Records the creation date of the admission process entry

selected_chunks.text:
(Software Requirement Specification) 
GU-SRS Page 1

Flow of ERP Solution(Version 3.0) 
1. Module Name-Admission Module 
1.1. Campaign Management 
1.1.1. Track the various campaigns run by the PR department. These could include 
purely internal campaigns run for initiatives among the students of the 
organization as well as marketing campaigns meant for interesting prospective 
students 
1.1.2. Permits campaign to run using one or more media channels like TV, radio, SMS 
,newspaper, bulletin boards etc. 
1.1.3. Enter the academy location programs covered 
1.2. Pre Admission Management 
1.2.1. Admission Notification for Online Application 
1.2.2. OTP based verification of mobile and email ID. 
1.3. Post Admission Management 
1.3.1. Option 1:Direct entry on restricted user privileges' and on
 restricted IP's. 
1.3.2. Option 2: student will fill the details and admission in-charge will approve
 the details 
1.3.3. Transformation of preadmission records to post-admission. 
1.4. Admission Enquiry Management 
1.4.1. Handle multiple prospective students in a single enquiry form but each 
prospective student will be assigned a separate enquiry id 
1.4.2. Record more than one program and batch of interest to a prospective student 
enquiring 
1.4.3. Record the prospective student details, exams passed or program studying in, 
details of siblings studying in the organization if any, details of parents, address for 
communication, whether the parent is an employee etc 
1.4.4. Follow up with enquiries. 
1.4.5. Direct Conversion of Enquiry to application and Application to Admission 
1.4.6. Application Registration by Prospect Student through online portal 
1.4.7. Conducting online entrance examination 
1.4.8. Allocation of students to various institutes. 
1.4.9. Registration Status Marking (Accept / Reject / Cancelled) 
1.4.10. Generate Merit list 
1.4.11. View / Edit Merit list

(Software Requirement Specification) 
GU-SRS Page 2 
1.4.12. Admission Process Management  Admission process management (round creation, 
reporting, document verification, fees submission, branch allotment, final confirmation) 
1.4.13. Admission Transaction Student wise Status 
1.4.14. Student Admission Page ( with Min Information/requirement ) 
1.4.15. Admission Confirmation Student wise / Bulk 
1.4.16. Offer Letter - Student Wise / Bulk  
1.4.17. Admission cancellation 
1.4.18. Generate Registration / Enrollment Number 
1.4.19. Student Information Entry Page ( Detailed) 
1.4.20. Mobile number linked with Aadhar card (student and parents) 
1.4.21. Progress bar in student login which shows status of on -going admission (in which 
phase student is currently in). 
1.4.22. Bulk Student Photo Upload Utility 
1.4.23. Provision for provisional admission and then confirmation to final admission 
1.4.24. Transformation of preadmission records to post-admission. 
1.4.25. Student Id card Generation 
1.4.26. Branch Change 
1.4.27. Admission Register Report and Admission Analytics 
1.4.28. Application Data Import Utility 
1.4.29. Integration with No Paper Forms 
1.4.30. Student Selected Field Report 
1.4.31. Reports…. 
1.5. Admission Dashboard 
1.5.1. Student /Faculty/Employee can request  and upload  certificates that he/she 
wants to get issued. Auto approval, manual approval; rejection and complete 
certificate/document issue management can be done. 
1.5.2. Generate document using document generator and Print the 
certificates/documents directly on Institute stationary or pre -defined format is also 
present. 
1.5.3. Documents handed and upload over at time of admission. 
1.5.4. These could include: Domicile Certificate, Birth Certificate, Mark Sheets, Entrance 
Exam Certificate 
2. Module Name-Fee Management 
2.1.1. Fees Master Setup - Receipt Type, Fees Head, Payment Category, Standard Fees.. 
Etc 
2.1.2. Multi User Support/Multiple Program/Multiple School 
2.1.3. Security rights on add modify delete export 
2.1.4. Multiple Students blocking unblocking. 
2.1.5. Student fee plan enrollment at the time of program enrollment.

api_endpoints: GET /api/v1/fees_master - Retrieve a list of all fee master entries.
POST /api/v1/fees_master - Create a new fee master entry.
GET /api/v1/students - Retrieve a list of all students.
POST /api/v1/students - Create a new student entry.
GET /api/v1/fee_plan - Retrieve a list of all fee plans.
POST /api/v1/fee_plan - Create a new fee plan for a student.
PATCH /api/v1/receipt/{id}/status - Update the status of a receipt.
GET /api/v1/receipts - Retrieve a list of all receipts.
POST /api/v1/receipts - Create a new receipt for a fee payment.
GET /api/v1/refunds - Retrieve a list of all refunds.
POST /api/v1/refunds - Create a new refund entry.
PATCH /api/v1/students/{id}/block - Block a student from fee payments.
PATCH /api/v1/students/{id}/unblock - Unblock a student from fee payments.
GET /api/v1/daily_collection_reports - Retrieve a list of daily collection reports.
POST /api/v1/daily_collection_reports - Create a new daily collection report.

business_rules.description: Fees must be set up with a receipt type, fees head, payment category, and standard fees.
Support for multiple users, programs, and schools must be implemented.
Security rights must be enforced for adding, modifying, deleting, and exporting fee records.
Students can be blocked or unblocked from fee payments.
Students must be enrolled in a fee plan at the time of program enrollment.
Reports must be generated for outstanding fees and collections.
Refunds and cheque bounce handling must be implemented.
Duplicate receipt handling must be managed.
Admin must have the facility to update clearance amounts.
Demand generation for fees must be implemented.
Receipt collection must categorize fees into academic, exam, other, and fines.
Write-off fees must be recorded.
Scholarship entries must be managed.
Refunds must be processed with rules defined.
Refund entries must be recorded receipt-wise and without receipt.
Funds can be transferred between fee heads.
Daily collection reports must be generated.
Student ledgers must be maintained.
Fee head-wise collection summary reports must be generated.
The fees module must be linked with all academic and exam activities.
Sponsored and student invoicing must be managed.
Discounts on exam fees must be applied for identified students.
Integration with Tally must be established.

tables.description: Stores the master setup for fees including receipt type, fees head, payment category, and standard fees.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each fee master entry.

columns.name: receipt_type
columns.justification: Defines the type of receipt for the fee.

columns.name: fees_head
columns.justification: Specifies the head under which the fee is categorized.

columns.name: payment_category
columns.justification: Categorizes the payment type for the fee.

tables.description: Stores student information.
Links students to their fee plans.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each fee plan.

columns.name: student_id
columns.justification: References the student enrolled in the fee plan.

columns.name: fees_master_id
columns.justification: References the fee master setup for the plan.

columns.name: enrollment_date
columns.justification: Date when the student enrolled in the fee plan.

tables.description: Records fee payment receipts.

columns.name: student_id
columns.justification: References the student who made the payment.

columns.name: fees_master_id
columns.justification: References the fee master setup for the payment.

columns.name: receipt_date
columns.justification: Date when the payment was made.

tables.description: Records refund transactions for receipts.

columns.name: receipt_id
columns.justification: References the receipt for which the refund is issued.

columns.name: refund_date
columns.justification: Date when the refund was processed.

tables.description: Records scholarship details for students.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each scholarship entry.

columns.name: student_id
columns.justification: References the student receiving the scholarship.

columns.name: awarded_date
columns.justification: Date when the scholarship was awarded.

tables.description: Records write-off entries for fees.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each write-off entry.

columns.name: fees_master_id
columns.justification: References the fee master setup for the write-off.

columns.name: write_off_date
columns.justification: Date when the write-off was processed.

tables.description: Records fund transfers between fee heads.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each fund transfer.

columns.name: from_fees_head
columns.justification: References the fee head from which funds are transferred.

columns.name: to_fees_head
columns.justification: References the fee head to which funds are transferred.

columns.name: transfer_date
columns.justification: Date when the transfer was made.

tables.description: Stores daily collection reports.

columns.name: total_collected
columns.justification: Total amount collected on the report date.

columns.name: total_outstanding
columns.justification: Total outstanding amount on the report date.

tables.description: Maintains a ledger for each student.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each ledger entry.

columns.name: student_id
columns.justification: References the student for whom the ledger is maintained.

columns.name: total_due
columns.justification: Total amount due from the student.

columns.name: total_paid
columns.justification: Total amount paid by the student.

columns.name: last_updated
columns.justification: Date when the ledger was last updated.

tables.description: Stores clearance amount updates made by admin.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each clearance amount entry.

columns.name: admin_id
columns.justification: References the admin who updated the clearance amount.

columns.name: update_date
columns.justification: Date when the clearance amount was updated.

tables.description: Manages duplicate receipt handling.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each duplicate receipt entry.

columns.name: receipt_id
columns.justification: References the original receipt that is duplicated.

columns.name: duplicate_receipt_date
columns.justification: Date when the duplicate receipt was created.

columns.name: reason
columns.justification: Reason for handling the duplicate receipt.

tables.description: Handles demand generation for fees.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each demand generation entry.

columns.name: student_id
columns.justification: References the student for whom the demand is generated.

columns.name: fees_master_id
columns.justification: References the fee master setup for the demand.

columns.name: demand_date
columns.justification: Date when the demand was generated.

tables.description: Manages invoicing for sponsored and student payments.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each invoicing entry.

columns.name: student_id
columns.justification: References the student for whom the invoice is generated.

columns.name: invoice_date
columns.justification: Date when the invoice was created.

columns.name: sponsor_details
columns.justification: Details of the sponsor for the invoice.

tables.description: Manages discounts on exam fees for identified students.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each discount entry.

columns.name: student_id
columns.justification: References the student receiving the discount.

columns.name: exam_fee_id
columns.justification: References the exam fee to which the discount applies.

columns.name: discount_date
columns.justification: Date when the discount was applied.

tables.description: Handles integration with Tally for financial transactions.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each Tally integration entry.

columns.name: transaction_id
columns.justification: Identifier for the transaction being integrated.

columns.name: tally_status
columns.justification: Status of the transaction in Tally.

columns.name: error_message
columns.justification: Error message if the integration fails.

tables.description: Stores rules for processing refunds.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each refund rule.

columns.name: rule_description
columns.justification: Description of the refund processing rule.

columns.name: created_date
columns.justification: Date when the refund rule was created.

tables.description: Stores fee head-wise collection summary reports.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each collection summary entry.

columns.name: fees_head
columns.justification: Specifies the head under which the fee is categorized.

columns.name: total_collected
columns.justification: Total amount collected under the specified fee head.

tables.description: Links fees with academic and exam activities.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each link entry.

columns.name: fees_master_id
columns.justification: References the fee master setup linked to academic or exam activities.

columns.name: activity_id
columns.justification: Identifier for the specific academic or exam activity.

design.sql_ddl:
-- Description: Table to store academic calendar details.
CREATE TABLE academic_calendar (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY, -- SRS: Unique identifier for each academic calendar.
    name string NOT NULL, -- SRS: Name of the academic calendar.
    start_date date NOT NULL, -- SRS: Start date of the academic calendar.
    end_date date NOT NULL, -- SRS: End date of the academic calendar.
    description string NULL, -- SRS: Description of the academic calendar.
    working_days json NOT NULL, -- SRS: JSON object defining working days.
    holidays json NOT NULL, -- SRS: JSON object defining holidays.
    special_days json NOT NULL -- SRS: JSON object defining special days.
);
CREATE INDEX idx_academic_calendar_name ON academic_calendar(name);

-- Description: Table to store events linked to academic calendars.
CREATE TABLE event (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY, -- SRS: Unique identifier for each event.
    title string NOT NULL, -- SRS: Title of the event.
    event_date date NOT NULL, -- SRS: Date of the event.
    event_type string NOT NULL, -- SRS: Type of the event (e.g., seminar, guest lecture).
    description string NULL, -- SRS: Description of the event.
    calendar_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES academic_calendar(id) -- SRS: Foreign key linking to the academic calendar.
);
CREATE INDEX idx_event_title ON event(title);

-- Description: Table to store schedules for assignments and assessments.
CREATE TABLE schedule (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY, -- SRS: Unique identifier for each schedule.
    calendar_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES academic_calendar(id), -- SRS: Foreign key linking to the academic calendar.
    schedule_type string NOT NULL, -- SRS: Type of schedule (e.g., assignment, examination).
    schedule_date date NOT NULL, -- SRS: Date of the schedule.
    details string NULL -- SRS: Details of the schedule.
);
CREATE INDEX idx_schedule_calendar_id ON schedule(calendar_id);

-- Description: Table to define work timings for each academic calendar on each working day.
CREATE TABLE calendar_work_timings (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY, -- SRS: Unique identifier for each work timing entry.
    calendar_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES academic_calendar(id), -- SRS: Foreign key linking to the academic calendar.
    day_of_week string NOT NULL, -- SRS: Day of the week for which the work timings are defined.
    start_time time NOT NULL, -- SRS: Start time of work on the specified day.
    end_time time NOT NULL, -- SRS: End time of work on the specified day.
    break_start_time time NULL, -- SRS: Start time of the break during work hours.
    break_end_time time NULL -- SRS: End time of the break during work hours.
);
CREATE INDEX idx_calendar_work_timings_calendar_id ON calendar_work_timings(calendar_id);
api_endpoints: GET /api/v1/academic_calendars - Retrieve a list of academic calendars.
POST /api/v1/academic_calendars - Create a new academic calendar.
GET /api/v1/academic_calendars/{id} - Retrieve a specific academic calendar by ID.
PATCH /api/v1/academic_calendars/{id} - Update an existing academic calendar.
DELETE /api/v1/academic_calendars/{id} - Delete an academic calendar.
GET /api/v1/academic_calendars/{calendar_id}/events - Retrieve events linked to a specific academic calendar.
POST /api/v1/academic_calendars/{calendar_id}/events - Create a new event linked to an academic calendar.
GET /api/v1/academic_calendars/{calendar_id}/schedules - Retrieve schedules linked to a specific academic calendar.
POST /api/v1/academic_calendars/{calendar_id}/schedules - Create a new schedule linked to an academic calendar.
GET /api/v1/academic_calendars/{calendar_id}/work_timings - Retrieve work timings for a specific academic calendar.
POST /api/v1/academic_calendars/{calendar_id}/work_timings - Define work timings for a specific academic calendar.

business_rules.description: Each academic calendar must define working days, holidays, and special days.
Work timings must be specified for each calendar on working days.
Schedules for assignments, internal assessments, and examinations must be defined.
Events such as guest lectures and seminars must be scheduled and linked to calendars.

api_endpoints: GET /api/v1/courses - Retrieve a list of all courses.
POST /api/v1/courses - Create a new course.
GET /api/v1/students - Retrieve a list of all students.
POST /api/v1/students - Create a new student.
GET /api/v1/faculty - Retrieve a list of all faculty members.
POST /api/v1/faculty - Create a new faculty member.
PATCH /api/v1/course_registration/{id}/approve - Approve course registration by Head of Department.
PATCH /api/v1/course_registration/{id}/drop - Drop a course registration.
PATCH /api/v1/attendance/{id}/mark - Record attendance for a class session.
PATCH /api/v1/internal_marks/{id}/lock - Lock internal marks through Head MIS account.
PATCH /api/v1/internal_marks/{id}/unlock - Unlock internal marks through Head MIS account.

business_rules.description: Each course must have a defined syllabus, course objectives, and outcomes.
Students must register for courses within a defined time frame.
Attendance must be recorded for each class session.
Internal marks must be locked/unlocked through the Head MIS account.
Course registration must be approved by the Head of Department.

api_endpoints: GET /api/v1/timetable - Retrieve a list of all timetable entries.
POST /api/v1/timetable - Create a new timetable entry.
PATCH /api/v1/timetable/{id}/status - Update the status of a timetable entry.
GET /api/v1/course - Retrieve a list of all courses.
POST /api/v1/course - Create a new course.
GET /api/v1/faculty - Retrieve a list of all faculty members.
POST /api/v1/faculty - Create a new faculty member.
GET /api/v1/room - Retrieve a list of all rooms.
POST /api/v1/room - Create a new room.
GET /api/v1/attendance_extra_lectures - Retrieve a list of all attendance records for extra lectures.
POST /api/v1/attendance_extra_lectures - Record attendance for an extra lecture.

business_rules.description: Timetable must accommodate all classes, faculty, and resources without conflicts.
In case of faculty leave, lectures must be transferred to available faculty.
Attendance must be recorded for extra lectures and practicals conducted by substitute faculty.

data_model.mermaid_er:
erDiagram
  TIMETABLE {
    uuid id PK
    uuid course_id FK
    uuid faculty_id FK
    uuid room_id FK
    timestamp start_time
    timestamp end_time
    string type
    string status
  }
  COURSE {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string description
  }
  FACULTY {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string email
  }
  ROOM {
    uuid id PK
    string room_number
    string building
  }
  TIMETABLE ||--o{ COURSE : includes
  TIMETABLE ||--o{ FACULTY : assigned_to
  TIMETABLE ||--o{ ROOM : scheduled_in

design.er_diagram_mermaid:
erDiagram
  STUDENT {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string email
    string registration_number
  }
  COURSE {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string code
    integer credits
  }
  SECTION {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    uuid course_id FK
  }
  ATTENDANCE {
    uuid id PK
    uuid student_id FK
    uuid section_id FK
    date attendance_date
    enum status {'Present', 'Absent', 'Late'}
    integer marks
  }
  FACULTY {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string email
  }
  EXTRA_LECTURE {
    uuid id PK
    uuid faculty_id FK
    uuid section_id FK
    date lecture_date
    string topic
  }
  ATTENDANCE_REPORT {
    uuid id PK
    uuid section_id FK
    date report_date
    integer total_present
    integer total_absent
  }
  SECTION ||--o{ ATTENDANCE : records
  STUDENT ||--o{ ATTENDANCE : marks
  COURSE ||--o{ SECTION : contains
  FACULTY ||--o{ EXTRA_LECTURE : conducts
  SECTION ||--o{ EXTRA_LECTURE : includes
  SECTION ||--o{ ATTENDANCE_REPORT : generates
api_endpoints: GET /api/v1/students - Retrieve a list of all students.
POST /api/v1/students - Create a new student record.
GET /api/v1/courses - Retrieve a list of all courses.
POST /api/v1/courses - Create a new course record.
GET /api/v1/sections - Retrieve a list of all sections.
POST /api/v1/sections - Create a new section record.
GET /api/v1/attendance - Retrieve attendance records for students.
POST /api/v1/attendance - Record attendance for a student.
PATCH /api/v1/attendance/{id} - Update attendance status for a student.
GET /api/v1/extra-lectures - Retrieve a list of all extra lectures.
POST /api/v1/extra-lectures - Create a new extra lecture record.
GET /api/v1/attendance-reports - Retrieve attendance reports for sections.
POST /api/v1/attendance-reports - Generate a new attendance report.
PATCH /api/v1/attendance-approval/{id} - Update attendance approval status.

business_rules.description: Attendance must be recorded for each student in a course section.
Attendance reports must be generated daily, weekly, and monthly.
Attendance must be captured for extra lectures and practicals.
Attendance must be approved by the MIS Head for lagging attendance.
Practical performance marks must be recorded alongside attendance.
Attendance must be captured through mobile applications.

tables.description: Stores information about students.

columns.name: registration_number
columns.justification: Unique registration number for the student.

tables.description: Stores information about courses offered.

columns.name: credits
columns.justification: Number of credits assigned to the course.

tables.description: Stores information about course sections.
Records attendance for students in sections.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each attendance record.

tables.description: Stores information about faculty members.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each faculty member.

columns.name: email
columns.justification: Email address of the faculty member.

tables.description: Records extra lectures conducted by faculty.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each extra lecture.

columns.name: faculty_id
columns.justification: Foreign key referencing the faculty member.

columns.name: topic
columns.justification: Topic covered in the extra lecture.

tables.description: Stores attendance reports for sections.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each attendance report.

tables.description: Records attendance for students in extra lectures.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each extra lecture attendance record.

columns.name: attendance_id
columns.justification: Foreign key referencing the attendance record.

columns.name: extra_lecture_id
columns.justification: Foreign key referencing the extra lecture.

columns.name: approval_status
columns.justification: Status of attendance approval by the MIS Head.

tables.description: Records attendance for students in audit courses.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each audit course attendance record.

columns.name: attendance_id
columns.justification: Foreign key referencing the attendance record.

columns.name: course_id
columns.justification: Foreign key referencing the audit course.

tables.description: Records attendance approval by the MIS Head for lagging attendance.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each attendance approval record.

columns.name: attendance_id
columns.justification: Foreign key referencing the attendance record.

columns.name: approval_status
columns.justification: Status of attendance approval by the MIS Head.

columns.name: approval_date
columns.justification: Date when the attendance was approved.

api_endpoints: GET /api/v1/students - Retrieve a list of all students.
POST /api/v1/students - Create a new student record.
GET /api/v1/examinations - Retrieve a list of all examinations.
POST /api/v1/examinations - Create a new examination record.
GET /api/v1/exam_centers - Retrieve a list of all exam centers.
POST /api/v1/exam_centers - Create a new exam center record.
GET /api/v1/hall_tickets - Retrieve a list of all hall tickets.
POST /api/v1/hall_tickets - Create a new hall ticket record.
GET /api/v1/attendances - Retrieve a list of all attendance records.
POST /api/v1/attendances - Create a new attendance record.
GET /api/v1/marks_entries - Retrieve a list of all marks entries.
POST /api/v1/marks_entries - Create a new marks entry record.
GET /api/v1/grace_policies - Retrieve a list of all grace policies.
POST /api/v1/grace_policies - Create a new grace policy record.
PATCH /api/v1/hall_tickets/{id}/generate - Generate hall ticket for a student.
PATCH /api/v1/attendances/{id}/mark - Update attendance status for a student.
PATCH /api/v1/marks_entries/{id}/update - Update marks entry for a student.

business_rules.description: Each student must be allocated to an exam center for each examination.
Exam papers must be finalized before the exam date.
Students must fill out examination forms online.
Hall tickets must be generated for each student before the examination.
Attendance must be recorded for each examination.
Marks must be entered for internal and external assessments.
Grace policies must be configured for students.

tables.description: Stores information about students.

columns.name: registration_number
columns.justification: Unique registration number for each student.

columns.justification: Foreign key referencing the exam center.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each exam center.

tables.description: Stores hall ticket information for students.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each hall ticket.

columns.name: ticket_number
columns.justification: Unique ticket number for the hall ticket.

tables.description: Records attendance for examinations.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each attendance record.

columns.name: hall_ticket_id
columns.justification: Foreign key referencing the hall ticket.

columns.name: present
columns.justification: Indicates if the student was present.

tables.description: Stores marks for internal and external assessments.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each marks entry.

tables.description: Stores grace marks policies for students.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each grace policy.

tables.description: Stores information about invigilators.

columns.name: employee_id
columns.justification: Unique employee ID of the invigilator.

tables.description: Stores details of exam papers.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each exam paper.

columns.justification: Unique identifier for each seating arrangement.

columns.name: hall_ticket_id
columns.justification: Foreign key referencing the hall ticket.

columns.justification: Seat number assigned to the student.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each exam schedule.

tables.description: Stores information about paper valuers.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each paper valuer.

columns.name: exam_paper_id
columns.justification: Foreign key referencing the exam paper.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each malpractice report.

tables.description: Stores information about the printing of mark sheets.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each mark sheet printing record.

columns.justification: Duration of the invigilation in minutes.

design.er_diagram_mermaid:
erDiagram
  STUDENT {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string email
    string phone
    date date_of_birth
    string enrollment_number
  }
  COMPANY {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string industry_type
    string location
    string contact_person
    string contact_email
    string contact_phone
  }
  INTERNSHIP_REQUEST {
    uuid id PK
    uuid student_id FK
    uuid company_id FK
    date application_date
    enum status CHECK (status IN ('PENDING', 'APPROVED', 'REJECTED'))
    string joining_letter_url
    string completion_certificate_url
    date start_date
    date end_date
    integer total_days
  }
  PLACEMENT_DRIVE {
    uuid id PK
    uuid company_id FK
    date drive_date
    string venue
    string criteria
  }
  JOB_POSTING {
    uuid id PK
    uuid company_id FK
    string job_title
    string job_description
    date posting_date
    date application_deadline
  }
  RECRUITMENT_PROCESS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid job_posting_id FK
    string selection_criteria
    string process_steps
  }
  PLACEMENT_ANALYSIS_REPORT {
    uuid id PK
    date report_date
    string report_details
  }
api_endpoints: GET /api/v1/students - Retrieve a list of all students.
POST /api/v1/students - Create a new student record.
GET /api/v1/companies - Retrieve a list of all companies.
POST /api/v1/companies - Create a new company record.
GET /api/v1/internship_requests - Retrieve a list of all internship requests.
POST /api/v1/internship_requests - Create a new internship request.
PATCH /api/v1/internship_requests/{id}/status - Update the status of an internship request.
GET /api/v1/placement_drives - Retrieve a list of all placement drives.
POST /api/v1/placement_drives - Create a new placement drive.
GET /api/v1/job_postings - Retrieve a list of all job postings.
POST /api/v1/job_postings - Create a new job posting.
PATCH /api/v1/student_company_approval/{id}/status - Update the approval status of a company added by a student.

business_rules.description: Internship duration can be configured to six weeks, six months, or one year.
Students can apply for multiple companies through the Internship Request Form.
Companies added by students must be approved or rejected by the III cell.
Departmental coordinators must confirm the company a student is supposed to join.
Students must upload joining letters and completion certificates after internships.

tables.description: Stores information about students applying for internships.

columns.name: enrollment_number
columns.justification: Unique enrollment number for each student.

tables.description: Stores information about companies offering internships.

columns.name: industry_type
columns.justification: Type of industry the company belongs to.

columns.name: contact_phone
columns.justification: Contact phone number of the company.

tables.description: Tracks internship applications made by students.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each internship request.

columns.name: student_id
columns.justification: Reference to the student applying for the internship.

columns.name: company_id
columns.justification: Reference to the company offering the internship.

columns.name: application_date
columns.justification: Date when the application was submitted.

columns.name: status
columns.justification: Current status of the internship application.

columns.name: joining_letter_url
columns.justification: URL to the joining letter uploaded by the student.

columns.name: completion_certificate_url
columns.justification: URL to the completion certificate uploaded by the student.

columns.name: total_days
columns.justification: Total number of days calculated automatically.

tables.description: Stores information about placement drives conducted by companies.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each placement drive.

columns.name: company_id
columns.justification: Reference to the company conducting the drive.

columns.name: criteria
columns.justification: Criteria for selection during the drive.

tables.description: Stores job postings made by companies.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each job posting.

columns.name: company_id
columns.justification: Reference to the company making the job posting.

columns.name: job_title
columns.justification: Title of the job being posted.

columns.name: posting_date
columns.justification: Date when the job was posted.

tables.description: Details the recruitment process for job postings.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each recruitment process.

columns.name: job_posting_id
columns.justification: Reference to the job posting associated with the recruitment process.

columns.name: process_steps
columns.justification: Steps involved in the recruitment process.

tables.description: Stores reports generated for placement analysis.

columns.name: id

design.sql_ddl:
-- Description: Table to store alumni information.
CREATE TABLE alumni (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY, -- SRS: Unique identifier for each alumni.
    name string NOT NULL, -- SRS: Alumni's full name.
    email string UNIQUE NOT NULL, -- SRS: Alumni's email address.
    phone_number string UNIQUE, -- SRS: Alumni's contact number.
    graduation_year string NOT NULL, -- SRS: Year of graduation.
    degree string NOT NULL, -- SRS: Degree obtained by the alumni.
    major string NOT NULL, -- SRS: Major field of study.
    current_employer string, -- SRS: Current employer of the alumni.
    job_title string, -- SRS: Current job title of the alumni.
    profile_picture string, -- SRS: URL to the alumni's profile picture.
    bio string, -- SRS: Short biography of the alumni.
    status string CHECK (status IN ('active', 'inactive')) -- SRS: Status of the alumni profile.
);
CREATE INDEX idx_alumni_email ON alumni(email);

-- Description: Table to manage connections between alumni.
CREATE TABLE connection (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY, -- SRS: Unique identifier for each connection.
    alumni_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES alumni(id), -- SRS: ID of the alumni initiating the connection.
    connected_alumni_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES alumni(id), -- SRS: ID of the alumni being connected to.
    connected_at timestamp DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP -- SRS: Timestamp when the connection was made.
);
CREATE INDEX idx_connection_alumni_id ON connection(alumni_id);

-- Description: Table to store messages sent between alumni.
CREATE TABLE message (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY, -- SRS: Unique identifier for each message.
    sender_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES alumni(id), -- SRS: ID of the alumni sending the message.
    receiver_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES alumni(id), -- SRS: ID of the alumni receiving the message.
    content string NOT NULL, -- SRS: Content of the message.
    sent_at timestamp DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, -- SRS: Timestamp when the message was sent.
    is_read boolean DEFAULT FALSE -- SRS: Indicates if the message has been read.
);
CREATE INDEX idx_message_sender_id ON message(sender_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_message_receiver_id ON message(receiver_id);

-- Description: Table to store statistics related to alumni activity.
CREATE TABLE statistic (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY, -- SRS: Unique identifier for each statistic record.
    alumni_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES alumni(id), -- SRS: ID of the alumni for whom statistics are recorded.
    total_connections integer DEFAULT 0, -- SRS: Total number of connections made by the alumni.
    total_messages_sent integer DEFAULT 0, -- SRS: Total number of messages sent by the alumni.
    total_messages_received integer DEFAULT 0, -- SRS: Total number of messages received by the alumni.
    last_accessed timestamp -- SRS: Timestamp of the last access by the alumni.
);
CREATE INDEX idx_statistic_alumni_id ON statistic(alumni_id);
api_endpoints: GET /api/v1/alumni - Retrieve a list of all alumni profiles.
POST /api/v1/alumni - Create a new alumni profile.
PATCH /api/v1/alumni/{id}/status - Update the status of an alumni profile.
GET /api/v1/connections - Retrieve a list of all connections between alumni.
POST /api/v1/connections - Create a new connection between alumni.
GET /api/v1/messages - Retrieve a list of messages sent between alumni.
POST /api/v1/messages - Send a new message between alumni.
PATCH /api/v1/messages/{id}/read - Mark a message as read.
GET /api/v1/statistics - Retrieve statistics related to alumni activity.
POST /api/v1/statistics - Create statistics for an alumni.
PATCH /api/v1/statistics/{id} - Update statistics for an alumni.
GET /api/v1/news - Retrieve IT industry news and postings.

business_rules.description: Alumni must be able to create and manage their profiles.
Alumni can connect with other alumni and share experiences.
Alumni can access statistics related to the alumni pool.
Alumni can receive IT industry news and postings.
Alumni can send and receive messages through the mail system.

design.er_diagram_mermaid:
erDiagram
  FACULTY {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string email
    string department
  }
  RESEARCH_CONTRIBUTION {
    uuid id PK
    uuid faculty_id FK
    enum contribution_type
    string title
    date publication_date
    string details
  }
  RESEARCH_PROJECT {
    uuid id PK
    string project_name
    decimal budget
    uuid faculty_id FK
    enum status
    date start_date
    date end_date
  }
  RESEARCH_EVENT {
    uuid id PK
    string event_name
    date event_date
    string location
    uuid faculty_id FK
    enum participation_type
  }
  PUBLICATION {
    uuid id PK
    uuid faculty_id FK
    string title
    enum publication_type
    date publication_date
  }
  CONFERENCE {
    uuid id PK
    string conference_name
    date conference_date
    uuid faculty_id FK
    enum role
  }
  WORKSHOP {
    uuid id PK
    string workshop_name
    date workshop_date
    uuid faculty_id FK
    enum role
  }
api_endpoints: GET /api/v1/faculty - Retrieve a list of faculty members.
POST /api/v1/faculty - Create a new faculty member.
GET /api/v1/research_contributions - Retrieve a list of research contributions.
POST /api/v1/research_contributions - Create a new research contribution.
GET /api/v1/research_projects - Retrieve a list of research projects.
POST /api/v1/research_projects - Create a new research project.
PATCH /api/v1/research_projects/{id}/status - Update the status of a research project.
GET /api/v1/research_events - Retrieve a list of research events.
POST /api/v1/research_events - Create a new research event.
GET /api/v1/publications - Retrieve a list of publications.
POST /api/v1/publications - Create a new publication.
GET /api/v1/conferences - Retrieve a list of conferences.
POST /api/v1/conferences - Create a new conference entry.
GET /api/v1/workshops - Retrieve a list of workshops.
POST /api/v1/workshops - Create a new workshop entry.

business_rules.description: Each research contribution must be associated with a faculty member.
Research projects must have a defined budget and approval process.
All research contributions must be documented and quantifiable for appraisal purposes.

tables.description: Table to store faculty member details.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each faculty member.

columns.name: email
columns.justification: Email address of the faculty member.

tables.description: Table to store research contributions by faculty.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each research contribution.

columns.name: faculty_id
columns.justification: Reference to the faculty member contributing.

columns.name: contribution_type
columns.constraints: NOT NULL CHECK (contribution_type IN ('Paper', 'Book Review', 'Case Review', 'Case Study', 'Research Monograph', 'Conference Proceedings', 'Working Paper', 'Industry Report'))

tables.description: Table to manage research projects.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each research project.

columns.name: faculty_id
columns.justification: Reference to the faculty member managing the project.

columns.name: status
columns.justification: Current status of the research project.

tables.description: Table to store research events attended by faculty.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each research event.

columns.name: faculty_id
columns.justification: Reference to the faculty member attending the event.

columns.name: participation_type
columns.justification: Type of participation in the event.

tables.description: Table to store publications by faculty.

columns.name: faculty_id
columns.justification: Reference to the faculty member who published.

tables.description: Table to store conferences attended by faculty.

columns.name: faculty_id
columns.justification: Reference to the faculty member attending the conference.

columns.name: role
columns.justification: Role of the faculty member in the conference.

tables.description: Table to store workshops attended by faculty.

columns.name: faculty_id
columns.justification: Reference to the faculty member attending the workshop.

columns.name: role
columns.justification: Role of the faculty member in the workshop.

api_endpoints: GET /api/v1/complaint_categories - Retrieve a list of all complaint categories.
POST /api/v1/complaint_categories - Create a new complaint category.
GET /api/v1/complaints - Retrieve a list of all complaints.
POST /api/v1/complaints - Create a new complaint.
PATCH /api/v1/complaints/{id}/status - Update the status of a complaint.
GET /api/v1/reply_templates - Retrieve a list of all reply templates.
POST /api/v1/reply_templates - Create a new reply template.
GET /api/v1/telephonic_queries - Retrieve a list of all telephonic queries.
POST /api/v1/telephonic_queries - Log a new telephonic query.
GET /api/v1/alerts - Retrieve a list of all alerts.
POST /api/v1/alerts - Create a new alert.

business_rules.description: Each complaint category must have a defined escalation process with specific timelines.
Standard reply templates must be associated with specific complaint types.
Telephonic queries must be logged by designated personnel.
Alerts must be sent via email or SMS for specific events.

tables.description: Stores categories for complaints.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each complaint category.

columns.name: escalation_days
columns.justification: Number of days for escalation as per SRS.

tables.description: Stores individual complaints submitted by students.

columns.name: category_id
columns.justification: Foreign key linking to the complaint category.

columns.name: student_id
columns.justification: Identifier for the student submitting the complaint.

columns.name: created_at
columns.justification: Timestamp when the complaint was created.

columns.name: updated_at
columns.justification: Timestamp when the complaint was last updated.

tables.description: Stores standard reply templates for complaints.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each reply template.

columns.name: category_id
columns.justification: Foreign key linking to the complaint category.

columns.name: template
columns.justification: The content of the reply template.

columns.name: is_standard
columns.justification: Indicates if the template is a standard reply.

tables.description: Logs telephonic queries made by students.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each telephonic query.

columns.name: student_id
columns.justification: Identifier for the student making the query.

columns.name: logged_at
columns.justification: Timestamp when the query was logged.

tables.description: Stores alerts sent to students.

columns.name: medium
columns.justification: Medium through which the alert is sent.

columns.name: sent_at
columns.justification: Timestamp when the alert was sent.

12.1. Admin Login 
12.1.1. System has option to create categories and sub categories under which 
complaints have to be accepted 
12.1.2. For each category and sub category system should provide configuration option 
for the to be accepted. following items 
12.1.2.1. Answered by 
12.1.2.2. Escalation Level 1 
12.1.2.3. Escalation Level 2 (optional) 
12.1.2.4. Reviewer (optional) 
12.1.2.5. Response from student after resolution (optional) 
For each of these, number of working days in which escalation will be done has to 
be captured. 
12.2. Standard reply templates 
12.2.1. System allows storing of standard reply templates with respect to each complaint 
type / category. 
12.2.2. User can optionally select replies from the template. 
12.2.3. System should filter reply templates according to the complaint type. 
12.2.4. Every time while replying the query replier should have to select one of them 
12.2.4.1. Response 
12.2.4.2. Resolution

12.3.1. Login required to capture telephonic queries by designated person, behavior 
should be like the student query.7

authorities Only after punching at all heads, consolidated clearance report will be 
generated in student login.

design.sql_ddl:
-- Description: Stores employee details.
CREATE TABLE employee (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.1
    first_name string NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.2
    middle_name string, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.2
    last_name string NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.2
    email string UNIQUE NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.3
    phone string NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.3
    permanent_address string NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.3
    contact_address string NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.3
    date_of_birth date NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.5
    date_of_joining date NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.6
    status enum('probation', 'confirmed', 'resigned', 'terminated', 'suspended', 'deputation', 'retired', 'reemployed') NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.7
    educational_qualification string, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.9
    community_details string, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.10
    gender string NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.11
    photo string, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.4
    probation_start_date date, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.52
    probation_end_date date, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.52
    department string, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.20
    designation string, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.21
    grade string, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.21
    reporting_hierarchy string, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.23
    pan_number string, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.24
    bank_account_details string, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.37
    liabilities string, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.17
    achievements string, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.34
    warnings string, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.34
    training_details string, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.36
    publications string -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.29
);
CREATE INDEX idx_employee_email ON employee(email);

-- Description: Stores leave applications by employees.
CREATE TABLE leave (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 15.21
    employee_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES employee(id), -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 15.21
    leave_type string NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 15.21
    start_date date NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 15.21
    end_date date NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 15.21
    status enum('pending', 'approved', 'rejected') NOT NULL -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 15.21
);
CREATE INDEX idx_leave_employee_id ON leave(employee_id);

-- Description: Stores salary details for employees.
CREATE TABLE salary (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 14.1
    employee_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES employee(id), -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 14.1
    basic_pay decimal(10,2) NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 14.1
    hra decimal(10,2) NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 14.1
    gross_pay decimal(10,2) NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 14.1
    deductions decimal(10,2) NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 14.1
    net_pay decimal(10,2) NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 14.1
    payment_date date NOT NULL -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 14.1
);
CREATE INDEX idx_salary_employee_id ON salary(employee_id);

-- Description: Stores performance evaluations for employees.
CREATE TABLE performance (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 16.1
    employee_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES employee(id), -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 16.1
    evaluation_date date NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 16.1
    score decimal(5,2) NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 16.1
    comments string -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 16.1
);
CREATE INDEX idx_performance_employee_id ON performance(employee_id);

-- Description: Stores contractual employee details.
CREATE TABLE contract (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.1.9
    employee_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES employee(id), -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.1.9
    contract_start_date date NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.1.9
    contract_end_date date NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.1.9
    contract_terms string -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.1.9
);
CREATE INDEX idx_contract_employee_id ON contract(employee_id);

-- Description: Stores documents related to employees.
CREATE TABLE document (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.1.10
    employee_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES employee(id), -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.1.10
    document_type string NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.1.10
    document_path string NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.1.10
    upload_date date NOT NULL -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.1.10
);
CREATE INDEX idx_document_employee_id ON document(employee_id);

-- Description: Stores details of employee liabilities.
CREATE TABLE employee_liability (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.17
    employee_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES employee(id), -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.17
    liability_type string NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.17
    amount decimal(10,2) NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.17
    due_date date -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.17
);

-- Description: Stores immediate family members of employees.
CREATE TABLE employee_family_member (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.27
    employee_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES employee(id), -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.27
    relation string NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.27
    name string NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.27
    photo string -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.27
);

-- Description: Stores employee achievements and warnings.
CREATE TABLE employee_achievement_warning (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.34
    employee_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES employee(id), -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.34
    achievement string, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.34
    warning string, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.34
    date date NOT NULL -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.34
);

-- Description: Stores health details of employees.
CREATE TABLE employee_health (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.45
    employee_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES employee(id), -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.45
    blood_group string NOT NULL, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.45
    identification_marks string, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.45
    consulting_hospital string -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.45
);

-- Description: Stores reporting hierarchy of employees.
CREATE TABLE employee_reporting_hierarchy (
    id uuid PRIMARY KEY, -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.23
    employee_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES employee(id), -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.23
    supervisor_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES employee(id) -- SRS: GU-SRS Page 19, 13.2.23
);
api_endpoints: GET /api/v1/employees - Retrieve a list of all employees.
POST /api/v1/employees - Create a new employee.
PATCH /api/v1/employees/{id}/status - Update the status of an employee.
GET /api/v1/leaves - Retrieve a list of all leave applications.
POST /api/v1/leaves - Create a new leave application.
PATCH /api/v1/leaves/{id}/status - Update the status of a leave application.
GET /api/v1/salaries - Retrieve a list of all salary details.
POST /api/v1/salaries - Create a new salary record.
GET /api/v1/performance - Retrieve a list of all performance evaluations.
POST /api/v1/performance - Create a new performance evaluation.

business_rules.description: An employee's probation period will be increased if they take leave during the probation.
An employee must be uniquely identified by a unique number.
An employee's status can be probation, confirmed, resigned, terminated, suspended, deputation, retired, or reemployed.

be captured.

api_endpoints: GET /api/v1/journal_vouchers - Retrieve a list of journal vouchers.
POST /api/v1/journal_vouchers - Create a new journal voucher.
GET /api/v1/expense_vouchers - Retrieve a list of expense vouchers.
POST /api/v1/expense_vouchers - Create a new expense voucher.
GET /api/v1/payment_vouchers - Retrieve a list of payment vouchers.
POST /api/v1/payment_vouchers - Create a new payment voucher.
GET /api/v1/receipt_vouchers - Retrieve a list of receipt vouchers.
POST /api/v1/receipt_vouchers - Create a new receipt voucher.
GET /api/v1/debit_credit_notes - Retrieve a list of debit and credit notes.
POST /api/v1/debit_credit_notes - Create a new debit or credit note.
GET /api/v1/invoices - Retrieve a list of invoices.
POST /api/v1/invoices - Create a new invoice.
GET /api/v1/customers - Retrieve a list of customers.
POST /api/v1/customers - Create a new customer.
GET /api/v1/suppliers - Retrieve a list of suppliers.
POST /api/v1/suppliers - Create a new supplier.
GET /api/v1/bank_reconciliations - Retrieve a list of bank reconciliations.
POST /api/v1/bank_reconciliations - Create a new bank reconciliation.
GET /api/v1/budgets - Retrieve a list of budgets.
POST /api/v1/budgets - Create a new budget.
GET /api/v1/audit_reports - Retrieve a list of audit reports.
POST /api/v1/audit_reports - Create a new audit report.

business_rules.description: All financial transactions must be recorded with appropriate vouchers.
All accounts receivable and payable must be tracked and reconciled.
All financial reports must be generated based on the latest data.
Budget must be compared against actual expenditures.

tables.description: Table to store journal vouchers.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each journal voucher.

columns.justification: Type of the journal voucher (e.g., Journal, Party).

columns.name: created_at
columns.justification: Date when the voucher was created.

tables.description: Table to store expense vouchers.

columns.name: id
columns.justification: Unique identifier for each expense voucher.

columns.justification: Reference to the associated journal voucher.

columns.justification: Date when the expense voucher was created.

columns.name: created_by
columns.justification: User who created the expense voucher.

tables.description: Table to store payment vouchers.

columns.justification: Reference to the associated journal voucher.

columns.justification: User who created the payment voucher.

tables.description: Table to store receipt vouchers.

columns.justification: Unique identifier for each receipt voucher.

columns.justification: Reference to the associated journal voucher.

columns.name: created_at
columns.justification: Date when the receipt voucher was created.

columns.name: created_by
columns.justification: User who created the receipt voucher.

tables.description: Table to store debit and credit notes.

columns.justification: Unique identifier for each debit/credit note.

columns.name: journal_voucher_id
columns.justification: Reference to the associated journal voucher.

columns.name: created_at
columns.justification: Date when the note was created.

tables.description: Table to store invoices.

columns.justification: Reference to the customer associated with the invoice.

Table to store supplier details.

(Software Requirement Specification) 
GU-SRS Page 33 
35.1. Create and upload journal vouchers, party journal vouchers. 
35.2. Upload expense vouchers, payment vouchers, receipt vouchers, debit/credit notes.  
35.3. Account receivables  - view sale invoices, create receipt vouchers, view inter unit 
transactions, clear customer balances, and prepare bank reconciliation statements.

design.er_diagram_mermaid:
erDiagram
  VOUCHER {
    uuid id PK
    string type
    date created_date
    date voucher_date
    string status
    uuid created_by
    uuid approved_by
  }
  EMPLOYEE {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string email
    string phone
  }
  REPORT {
    uuid id PK
    string report_type
    date generated_date
    uuid generated_by
  }
  ADVANCE {
    uuid id PK
    uuid employee_id
    decimal amount
    date request_date
    string status
  }
  VOUCHER ||--o{ EMPLOYEE : created_by
  VOUCHER ||--o{ EMPLOYEE : approved_by
  REPORT ||--o{ EMPLOYEE : generated_by
  ADVANCE ||--o{ EMPLOYEE : employee_id
api_endpoints: GET /api/v1/vouchers - Retrieve a list of all vouchers.
POST /api/v1/vouchers - Create a new voucher.
PATCH /api/v1/vouchers/{id}/status - Update the status of a voucher.
GET /api/v1/employees - Retrieve a list of all employees.
POST /api/v1/employees - Create a new employee.
GET /api/v1/reports - Retrieve a list of all reports.
POST /api/v1/reports - Generate a new report.
GET /api/v1/advances - Retrieve a list of all advance requests.
POST /api/v1/advances - Create a new advance request.
PATCH /api/v1/advances/{id}/status - Update the status of an advance request.

business_rules.description: All vouchers must be created, uploaded, and approved before they can be processed.
All financial reports must be generated based on the latest data available in the system.
All employee advances must be settled against receipts or salary.
All transactions must be recorded in the respective registers for audit purposes.
